top_insights: You don't lack discipline; you lack a routine that forces it.
Creator-led businesses solve real problems; corporations just sell status.
The biggest lie is that competition drives innovation.
If you don't design your life, someone else will.
True leverage isn't working harder; it's exposing the hidden truth.
angles: Expose the 'hidden truth' behind common misconceptions
Frame personal discipline as a strategic advantage
Highlight creator-led success stories as the new business model
Use strong contrasts to challenge status quo thinking
Position advice as exclusive leverage or status signals
emerging_narrative: Audiences are resonating with content that exposes hidden realities, validates their own struggles or insights, and provides actionable, high-leverage strategies for personal and professional growth. There is a strong preference for authentic, creator-driven solutions over traditional systems.

viral_examples.author: paulg
viral_examples.text: Every picture of JP Morgan published in his lifetime (and thus nearly every picture published since) was heavily retouched. This is what he actually looked like.

viral_examples.author: paulg
viral_examples.text: 65 doctors, nurses, and paramedics told the New York Times what they saw in Gaza. What they saw was a pattern of children being shot in the head.

viral_examples.author: gregisenberg
viral_examples.text: this youtuber with 9m subs saw his wife struggle to find a quality wheelchair — $5k price tags, long waits, made in china so he built his own factory in the USA now, he sells these $1,000 custom wheelchairs, shipped in weeks we need more creator-led businesses like this.

viral_examples.author: paulg
viral_examples.text: I don't have to tell you what happened to these three boys. You already know. How awful is that?

viral_examples.author: paulg
viral_examples.text: Which of America's bold new cohort of Republican leaders is bold enough to answer this?
